title: toxicodendron delavayi var. quinquejugum
intro: Toxicodendron delavayi var. quinquejugum, also known as the Five-leaved poison ivy, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family and is native to the Himalayas. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Toxicodendron delavayi var. quinquejugum include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and relieving respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although caution should be taken due to its toxic and allergenic properties.
